source_jobs.title: AFC & Card Fraud Intern (f/m/d)
source_jobs.description:
At Moss, we give finance professionals the power to automate their day-to-day and make forward-thinking decisions.

Our team and culture make us unique — we’re driven by impact and growth, where every one of us strives to learn and excel. Recognised by Sifted’s Rising 100 https://sifted.eu/rankings/b2b-saas-rising-100-2024 and LinkedIn's Top Startups https://www.linkedin.com/pulse/linkedin-top-startups-2024-20-aufstrebende-unternehmen-bjd0c/, we’re here to help propel your career and together, make Moss a lasting success.

Join our team at Moss as an AFC & Card Fraud Intern (f/m/d)

As a BaFin-regulated e-money institution, Compliance is core to how Moss operates, not a side function. In this role, you will support our Anti-Financial Crime (AFC) team across name screening, transaction monitoring, card fraud management and KYC/KYB, while also helping us automate these processes, incl. the use of AI tools. You will be at the center of modern Compliance teams’ work: understanding and ensuring the regulatory substance and facilitating improved and more automated processes by state-of-the art technology.

 


YOUR RESPONSIBILITIES

No day is the same at Moss, but here’s an overview of your core responsibilities:

 - Day-to-day AFC operations: You support transaction monitoring and spot checks, and contribute to chargeback and Mastercom case handling.

 - AI-powered automation: You help build and improve AI powered processes and prompts for recurring compliance tasks, develop workflows for reporting and alerting, and connect external APIs. You also work on iterating existing tools, for example our KYC 4-Eyes Check.

 - Cross-team collaboration: You work closely with KYC Ops, the Risk team and, where relevant, external partners such as Marqeta and Mastercard to make sure automation actually solves real operational problems.

 


ABOUT YOU

We think you’ll need the following experience and qualifications to succeed in this role:

 - You are studying Business, Law, Computer Science, Data Science, Mathematics or a related field; we care more about analytical rigor and curiosity than a specific degree.

 - You are fluent in English; German is a plus but not required.

 - First hands-on experience with AI tools is a strong plus.

 - You have a genuine interest in both regulatory/AFC topics and automation or coding; this role sits deliberately at the intersection of the two, not just one side of it.

In addition, here are the skills and attributes we are looking for:

 - Ownership: You take tasks from unclear to done, whether that's a case review or a half-finished automation, without needing every step handed to you.

 - AI fluency and curiosity: You want to understand what is actually happening behind a prompt or workflow, not just use it.

 - Structured and analytical: You keep track of detail across cases, data and rules, and nothing slips through the cracks.

 - Comfort with ambiguity: You are working at the intersection of regulation and engineering, often without a ready-made playbook, and you find that interesting rather than stressful.

Given the sensitivity of the data you will work with (customer and payment data), discretion and a careful handling of confidential information are expected throughout.

Location and setup

 - Based in Berlin, hybrid: 3 days in the office, 2 days from home.

 - Start: as soon as possible.

 - Duration: 6 months.

About Moss

Moss is a SaaS scale-up founded in Berlin, with a team of 300+ people from 50+ nationalities in 5 offices across Europe. 

Our ambition is bold: to power every SMB’s spend across Europe - fully digital, AI-driven, and seamlessly integrated for complete control. Moss is making a significant investment in AI tooling - and this role exists to turn that investment into measurable outcomes. As the first dedicated hire focused on agentic developer experience, you will build the tools, modular skills, guardrails, and infrastructure that help engineers across the company move faster and more safely with AI.

You will report to the Director of Engineering and sit within a Platform Engineering team of around eight people, working alongside Platform, Data, and AI Engineers.  - Build a developer productivity measurement platform integrating GitHub, Linear, Slack, and CI/CD signals into a coherent view of delivery flow

 - Identify and remove friction from engineering workflows: slow pipelines, poor feedback loops, unclear ownership, and noisy alerting

 - Define, deploy, and iterate on AI tooling for local development, including IDE integrations, agentic tools, MCP servers, and model integrations such as Claude and Codex

 - Build the guardrails that make AI adoption safe: prompt logging, data classification boundaries, an approved model catalogue, and usage observability

 - Understand the workflows of engineering and product teams to surface bottlenecks, systemic issues, and drive improvements with their buy-in.

 - Write documentation that engineers actually use: runbooks, adoption guides, and decision records

About you

 - 8+ years of experience in cloud-native startups, FinTechs, or high-volume engineering environments

 - 3+ years working in a team focused on developer experience

 - 1-2 years using modern agentic tools in a professional setting

 - 5+ years building integrations between services

 - Business fluent in English (C1 or above)

In addition, here are the skills and attributes we are looking for:

 - Comfortable navigating ambiguity and making decisions as new information emerges, without waiting for the problem to be fully defined

 - Able to identify what needs solving, not just execute what has been scoped

 - Brings rigour to fast-moving tooling decisions, weighing productivity gains against security and compliance constraints in a regulated environment

 - Builds trust with engineering teams through hands-on presence, not top-down mandates

source_jobs.title: Senior Information Security Manager (f/m/d)
source_jobs.description:
At Moss, we give finance professionals the power to automate their day-to-day and make forward-thinking decisions.

Our team and culture make us unique — we’re driven by impact and growth, where every one of us strives to learn and excel. Recognised by Sifted’s Rising 100 https://sifted.eu/rankings/b2b-saas-rising-100-2024 and LinkedIn's Top Startups https://www.linkedin.com/pulse/linkedin-top-startups-2024-20-aufstrebende-unternehmen-bjd0c/, we’re here to help propel your career and together, make Moss a lasting success.

Our Information Security team is seeking an Information Security GRC Lead (f/m/d). This role owns our security governance, risk, and compliance program - ensuring Moss meets its regulatory obligations as a BaFin-regulated EMI while enabling the business to move fast.

You'll report directly to the Director of Information Security. This is a senior individual contributor role with ownership and autonomy - no direct reports currently, but potential to grow the function over time.

We're looking for someone who treats GRC as an engineering problem, not a paperwork exercise. You'll drive automation, continuous control monitoring, and AI-assisted workflows to make compliance scalable and efficient.

What you'll own

 - Unified control framework - Build and maintain a single, unified control framework mapped to DORA, ISO 27001, SOC 2 Type 2, and GDPR. Each control should be defined once - with clear ownership, technical implementation details, and evidence sources - and mapped across all relevant standards.

 - ICT risk management - Own the ICT risk management framework and register (based on ISO 27005 or equivalent). Identify, assess, track, and report ICT risks. Collaborate with the Risk team to integrate ICT risks into the group-wide enterprise risk framework.

 - GRC automation - Automate everything you can: evidence collection, control testing, reporting, policy acknowledgements.

 - DORA compliance - Own the DORA compliance program: gap analysis, remediation tracking, ICT risk management framework.

 - Security incident management - Own security incident classification and regulatory reporting to BaFin (with CISO sign-off).

 - Business continuity - Own the BCM program, including BCP maintenance, testing, and BIA updates.

 - Audit readiness - Coordinate ISO 27001 and SOC 2 Type 2 audits end-to-end. Manage evidence collection, auditor relationships, and remediation tracking. Goal: continuous audit-readiness, not fire drills.

 - Asset and data classification - Own the classification schema and ensure assets and data are classified and maintained.

 - Security vendor assessments - Perform security due diligence on vendors and third-party applications.

 - Policy management - Own the security policy lifecycle: drafting, reviews, version control, stakeholder sign-off.

 - Security awareness - Own and run the security awareness program.

About you

 - You have built or run GRC programs in a fast-paced, regulated environment - ideally a financial institution or fintech.

 - You have hands-on experience with ISO 27001, SOC 2 Type 2, and GDPR. Experience with DORA or strong familiarity with its requirements is a plus.

 - You have built or managed unified control frameworks mapped across multiple standards - not separate control sets per audit, but one source of truth with cross-mappings.

 - You understand controls at the technical implementation level - not just "we have an access review policy" but how it's implemented, in which systems, and how evidence is collected.

 - You have designed or significantly evolved a risk management framework - whether based on ISO 27005, NIST, or a custom methodology. You understand how ICT risk integrates into enterprise risk management.

 - You have hands-on experience with GRC platforms (e.g. Vanta, Drata, ServiceNow GRC, or similar) - either implementing them or running mature processes on them.

 - You understand BaFin regulatory expectations or similar financial regulators

notes: Moss is a fintech company with a mix of tech and non-tech roles. They have a strong engineering core, but also a significant number of sales and customer success roles.
